_meta.description: Named entities extracted from Cyberpunk 2077 shards. Each entity has a type, faction affiliation, and degree of importance in the lore.

entities.name: Arasaka
entities.description: Japanese megacorporation specialising in security and military hardware.

entities.name: Militech
entities.description: American weapons and military technology megacorporation.

entities.name: Biotechnica
entities.description: Italian biotech megacorporation specialising in food and bioware.

entities.name: Petrochem
entities.description: Oil and chemical megacorporation with significant military interests.

entities.name: Night Corp
entities.description: The corporation that built and largely owns Night City infrastructure.

entities.name: MetaCorp
entities.description: A shadowy mid-tier corporation operating as a Arasaka/Militech proxy.

entities.name: Trauma Team
entities.description: Premium emergency medical response corporation. Subscription-based extraction.

entities.name: Dynalar Technologies
entities.description: Cyberware manufacturer, known for budget-tier neural implants.

entities.name: Kendachi
entities.description: Weapons manufacturer specialising in monoknife and blade technology.

entities.name: All Foods
entities.description: Synthetic food corporation. Secretly processes human biomass.

entities.name: Akebono
entities.description: Japanese tech brand known for arcade hardware and consumer electronics.

entities.name: Maelstrom
entities.description: Chrome-obsessed gang in Watson, infamous for extreme body modification.

entities.name: 6th Street
entities.description: Patriotic gang in Heywood, styled as American veterans.

entities.name: Voodoo Boys
entities.description: Elite netrunner gang in Pacifica with ties to the Old Net.

entities.name: Tyger Claws
entities.description: Japanese-influenced gang with Arasaka ties, controlling Japantown.

entities.name: Aldecaldos
entities.description: Major nomad clan, mobile and honour-bound, operating in the Badlands.

entities.name: NCPD
entities.description: Night City Police Department — underfunded, corrupt, outgunned.

entities.name: MaxTac
entities.description: NCPD's cyberpsycho response unit. Staffed by reformed psychos.

entities.name: Netwatch
entities.description: International Net policing agency, hunting rogue AIs and illegal netrunners.

entities.name: Night City Council
entities.description: Night City's corporate-controlled municipal government.

entities.name: NUSA
entities.description: New United States of America — the weakened federal government.

entities.name: FIA
entities.description: Federal Intelligence Agency — NUSA's intelligence service.

entities.name: Barghest
entities.description: Paramilitary force controlling Dogtown under Kurt Hansen.

entities.name: NorCal Armed Forces
entities.description: Northern California regional military force.

entities.name: NUSA Military
entities.description: NUSA's federal military apparatus.

entities.name: Samurai
entities.description: Legendary punk rock band fronted by Johnny Silverhand.

entities.name: Ripperdocs
entities.description: Black-market surgeons performing cyberware installation outside corp channels.

entities.name: Nomads
entities.description: Collective term for the mobile clans living outside Night City.

entities.name: Johnny Silverhand
entities.description: Iconic rockerboy and terrorist. Detonated Arasaka Tower in 2023. Soul stored on Relic.

entities.name: Alt Cunningham
entities.description: Johnny's former lover. Became a powerful AI after Arasaka captured her soul.

entities.name: Rache Bartmoss
entities.description: Greatest netrunner in history. Released DataKrash in 2022, shattering the Old Net.

entities.name: Saburo Arasaka
entities.description: Ancient CEO and patriarch of the Arasaka corporation.

entities.name: Yorinobu Arasaka
entities.description: Saburo's rebellious son, with anti-corporate sympathies turned ruthless ruler.

entities.name: Hanako Arasaka
entities.description: Saburo's loyal daughter, operating in the shadows of Arasaka politics.

entities.name: Kerry Eurodyne
entities.description: Former Samurai guitarist turned commercial rock star.

entities.name: Denny
entities.description: Samurai drummer, kept the band grounded.

entities.name: Henry
entities.description: Samurai bassist.

entities.name: Lizzy Wizzy
entities.description: Pop superstar who replaced her entire body with chrome. Wanted for murder.

entities.name: Liam Nolan
entities.description: Lizzy Wizzy's manager, killed by Lizzy in a psychotic break.

entities.name: Mr. Hands
entities.description: Pacifica fixer, calm and methodical operator.

entities.name: Regina Jones
entities.description: Watson fixer and journalist. Investigates cyberpsycho cases.

entities.name: Meredith Stout
entities.description: Militech agent, ruthless and efficient, hunts down corp leaks.

entities.name: Kurt Hansen
entities.description: Warlord commander of Barghest, rules Dogtown with iron control.

entities.name: Solomon Reed
entities.description: Deep-cover FIA operative stationed in Dogtown.

entities.name: Songbird
entities.description: NUSA netrunner asset operating in Dogtown under deep cover.

entities.name: President Myers
entities.description: President of the NUSA, trying to restore federal authority.

entities.name: Royce
entities.description: Maelstrom lieutenant who takes over from Brick. Volatile and paranoid.

entities.name: Brick
entities.description: Former Maelstrom leader. More pragmatic than Royce.

entities.name: Mathias Maddox
entities.description: MaxTac officer and author of 'The MaxTac Way'.

entities.name: 8ug8ear
entities.description: Netrunner and writer. Wrote the critical essay 'Blaming Bartmoss'.

entities.name: Tsutomu Takahashi
entities.description: Tech historian. Author of '75 Years of Cyberware'.

entities.name: Marlon Fuscino
entities.description: Night City resident who hired a hitman to avenge his daughter.

entities.name: Tucker Albach
entities.description: Hit-and-run driver, target of Marlon Fuscino's revenge.

entities.name: Hasan Demir
entities.description: Arasaka executive with suspicious financial activity.

entities.name: B.B. Pires
entities.description: Historian and author of 'A History of the Nomads'.

entities.name: Virginia Granchester
entities.description: Nomad writer, author of 'In the Shade of an Apple Tree'.

entities.name: Luke Steelman
entities.description: Sci-fi author, wrote 'Sayonara Station'.

entities.name: B.A. Guseinov
entities.description: Bioengineering researcher, published papers on cellular regeneration.
